agency: Office of the Queens Borough President (QBP)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting). queensbp.org 301-redirects to www.queensbp.nyc.gov; fingerprinted the target as WordPress (Divi theme + Elementor) on WP Engine behind Cloudflare, with Yoast SEO and Wordfence (via response headers, wp-json namespaces, generator/theme markup, and sitemap_index.xml). Confirmed the WordPress REST API (/wp-json/wp/v2) is enabled but effectively empty — X-WP-Total: 0 for both posts and the 'project' custom post type; content is authored as Divi Pages. Verified NYC Open Data via the Socrata Discovery API: Dataset-Information_Agency='Queens Borough President (QBP)' returns TWO datasets (community board members rps4-dwwk; district managers and chairs 8z5h-tzdr), both pulled with column schemas. Mapped site content areas from the page sitemap (land use, community boards, budget/major allocations, constituent services, newsroom).
headline_findings: queensbp.org 301-redirects to www.queensbp.nyc.gov — a WordPress/Divi brochure site on WP Engine behind Cloudflare (Yoast, Wordfence, Google Language Translator). A fifth distinct platform after Parks (Smarty/PHP), DOE (Sitefinity/.NET), Council (WordPress), and NYCHA (NYC.gov Livesite + Oracle Siebel).

The WordPress REST API (/wp-json/wp/v2) is switched ON but effectively EMPTY: 0 posts and 0 'project' custom-post-type entries. The one machine-readable surface the platform already ships is dormant because content is authored as Divi page-builder Pages, not structured posts.

Open Data is nearly absent but NOT zero: exactly TWO datasets attributed to 'Queens Borough President (QBP)', both about community boards — Queens Community Board Members (rps4-dwwk, 4 cols) and Queens Community Board District Managers and Chairs (8z5h-tzdr, 20 cols).

The core borough-president acts have NO machine-readable contract: advisory land-use (ULURP) recommendations, tens of millions in annual discretionary funding to nonprofits/schools/cultural groups, events, and press releases all live only as HTML.

Net-new write surface: apply to serve on a community board (CommunityBoardApplication) — the QBP's flagship participatory act ('2026 Queens Community Board Application Process'), today only an HTML/PDF form. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.
Reframe: all five borough presidents run near-identical thin sites. The real low-hanging fruit is ONE shared Borough President API standard implemented per borough, not five one-off builds.

apis_observed.note: Enabled and publicly reachable, returns valid JSON for pages, but X-WP-Total=0 for posts and the 'project' custom post type. A real API surface carrying almost no structured content. Namespaces include wp/v2, oembed, yoast/v1, divi/v1, wpe/cache-plugin, wordfence/v1.

Two QBP-attributed datasets (rps4-dwwk community board members; 8z5h-tzdr district managers and chairs). SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The only substantive machine-readable QBP data — both about community boards.
Land use, community boards, budget/major allocations, constituent services, newsroom — all Divi page-builder Pages; content only, no content API in practice.
301-redirects to www.queensbp.nyc.gov (Cloudflare); the office has migrated onto the nyc.gov domain.

meta.platform: WordPress (Divi + Elementor) on WP Engine behind Cloudflare; Yoast + Wordfence; queensbp.org 301s to www.queensbp.nyc.gov
meta.netnew: CommunityBoardApplication — apply to serve on a Queens community board (today an HTML/PDF form)
meta.tagline: A thin WordPress brochure with its REST API switched on but empty, and almost no open data — the fix is one shared Borough President API, not a fifth one-off.
meta.headline: Queens Borough President is a WordPress/Divi brochure whose one machine-readable surface (the WP REST API) is enabled but empty, with just two community-board datasets on Open Data — a candidate for one standardized API shared across all five borough presidents.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern managed WordPress/Divi on WP Engine + Cloudflare, but a page-builder brochure configuration
scorecard_notes.siteApi: WordPress REST API (wp/v2) is enabled and reachable but effectively empty (0 posts / 0 projects)
scorecard_notes.openData: Only two datasets, both community boards; the core BP functions have no open data
scorecard_notes.coreData: Land-use recs, discretionary funding, events, press are HTML-only; just community-board rosters are structured
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API — community board applications, land-use testimony, constituent requests are all email/HTML forms
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP or agent-native surface; wp-json exists generically but is not oriented to agents or populated
scorecard_notes.ownership: Migrated onto the nyc.gov domain and owns its CMS content, but the stack is commodity vendor SaaS (WP Engine, Divi, Cloudflare)

fruit.name: Community Boards (district managers & chairs)
opendata_match: Queens Community Board District Managers and Chairs 8z5h-tzdr (20c)
fruit.notes: The board office: district manager, chair, address, monthly meeting, and full NYC geography spine (BBL/BIN, council district, census tract, NTA, lat/long).

fruit.name: Community Board Members (appointments)
opendata_match: Queens Community Board District Managers and Chairs 8z5h-tzdr (20c)
fruit.notes: Who serves: first/last name, board, year appointed. The Borough President appoints all Queens CB members (half nominated by the local Council Member) per City Charter.

fruit.name: Land Use / ULURP recommendations
fruit.notes: The Borough President hosts Land Use Public Hearings and issues advisory recommendations (approve/disapprove) on ULURP applications. HTML/PDF only; no API, no QBP Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Discretionary funding (budget / major allocations)
fruit.notes: Tens of millions each year in capital and expense funding to nonprofits, schools, and cultural institutions. Application and awards are HTML-only; no API, no QBP Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Events (hearings, borough board/cabinet, expos, webinars)
fruit.notes: Land use hearings, Borough Board/Cabinet meetings, tech expo, affordable-housing webinars. Authored as Divi Pages; the WP REST API returns 0 events.

fruit.name: Newsroom (press releases, statements, reports)
fruit.notes: Runs on WordPress with the REST API enabled, yet wp/v2/posts returns 0 — releases are Divi Pages, so the platform's own machine-readable surface is dormant.

fruit.name: Apply to a community board
fruit.notes: The QBP's flagship participatory act — residents apply to be appointed to a community board (the '2026 Queens Community Board Application Process'). HTML/PDF form only; no API and no Open Data twin. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Constituent services request
fruit.notes: The Constituent Services Unit fields complaints about local government services and liaises with city agencies. Appointment/contact form only — an alternative net-new write surface alongside the community board application.
